principle: Cut checkout to 8 essential fields using five specific reductions — average sites show 14+ fields but optimal is 8, directly lifting conversion by up to 35%
explanation: X.ux|checkout_field_reduction: avg=14.88_fields; optimal=8; 5_cuts: (1)merge_First+Last->"Full_Name" (89%_dont; 42%_type_full_name_in_First_anyway) (2)hide_"Address_Line_2"_behind_expand_link (75%_dont; 30%_pause_on_it) (3)hide_coupon_code_behind_link (35%_show_by_default -> users_abandon_to_search_codes) (4)billing==shipping_checked_by_default+hidden (24%_show_separately) (5)account_creation_at_confirmation_step_only (84%_dont_delay); 18%_abandon_due_to_checkout_complexity; NO:splitting_name_fields NO:all_optional_fields_visible_by_default

principle: Show specific arrival dates in shipping options, not speed descriptions — users should not have to calculate what '4-7 business days' means for their calendar
explanation: X.ux|delivery_date_display: "4-7_business_days" -> user_opens_calendar+calculates -> cognitive_load + errors; show: "Arrives_April_4th" OR "April_4-7"; 48%_of_sites_dont_do_this; Apple_model: "Delivers_Thursday" -> zero_calculation; apply_to_every_shipping_option_row + on_product_page_below_price; NO:speed_only_without_date; eg: "Standard_(5-7_business_days)" >> "Standard — Arrives_by_Wed_April_9th" | "Express — Arrives_Thu_April_3rd"

principle: Product pages need five image types beyond the plain product shot — scale, lifestyle, texture, model, and customer photos each answer a different buying question
explanation: X.ux|product_image_types: plain_white_bg_only -> 42%_users_cant_judge_size; 5_required: (1)SCALE: product_next_to_hand/coin/known_object (37%_sites_skip) (2)LIFESTYLE: product_in_real_context (3)TEXTURE: extreme_close-up_of_material+seams (4)MODEL: human_wearing/holding (23%_skip_for_wearables) (5)UGC: customer_submitted_photos (most_trusted); also: pinch-zoom_on_mobile (40%_dont_support); high_res_required (25%_insufficient_zoom); NO:single_white_bg_image NO:zoom_that_pixelates

principle: Display product size and color variants as exposed button grids, not dropdowns — hiding options behind a dropdown stops comparison and drives abandonment
explanation: X.ux|variant_button_grid: dropdown_for_size+color -> options_hidden -> user_cant_compare; 57%_of_sites_use_dropdowns; exposed_button_grid: all_variants_visible -> availability_clear_at_glance; out_of_stock: show_with_strikethrough NOT hide -> user_sees_full_range+can_plan; color_swatches: squares_with_hover_name; selected_variant: clearly_highlighted; NO:dropdown_for_<10_variants NO:hiding_unavailable_sizes; eg: "Size: [Select dropdown]" >> "Size: [XS][S][M][L̶(OOS)][XL]"

principle: Write checkout error messages that name the exact format required and explain why sensitive fields are needed — generic 'invalid' messages force users to guess the fix
explanation: X.ux|checkout_adaptive_errors: 94%_of_sites_show_generic_errors; formula: [what_exactly_failed]+[how_to_fix]; phone: "Invalid_phone" >> "Phone_needs_10_digits,_no_spaces"; card: "Invalid_number" >> "Card_number_is_incomplete"; ZIP: "Invalid" >> "ZIP_must_be_5_digits"; sensitive_fields_need_why: phone_field -> "Used_only_to_verify_payment — never_shared" (70%_users_hesitate_giving_phone; 49%_of_sites_dont_explain); NO:"Invalid_input" NO:"Error_in_field" NO:sensitive_field_without_explanation

principle: Keep the complete order summary visible on every checkout step — 14% of users abandon when they cannot see the total they are committing to
explanation: X.ux|persistent_order_summary: order_summary_only_at_final_step -> user_lost_context_of_what_they_pay; 14%_abandon_because_cant_see_total; pattern: sticky_sidebar_desktop + collapsible_top_panel_mobile; shows: item_thumbnails + qty + subtotal + shipping(live_updated) + tax + TOTAL; update_in_real_time_as_shipping_option_changes; NO:order_total_hidden_until_review_step NO:order_summary_only_on_cart_page; eg: "checkout_form_no_total_visible" >> "checkout_form + sticky_sidebar: items+shipping+tax+total"

principle: Show return policy inline on the product page below the add-to-cart button — 60% of users look for it there and 15% abandon when they cannot find it
explanation: X.ux|return_policy_on_pdp: 60%_of_users_expect_returns_on_product_page; 15%_abandon_when_policy_invisible_or_unsatisfactory; 44%_of_sites_dont_show_it_on_PDP; placement: below_Add_to_Cart + above_full_description; format: one_line ("Free_30-day_returns") + link_to_full_policy; NOT_footer_only NOT_only_at_checkout; NO:return_policy_hidden_in_FAQ_page NO:first_mention_of_returns_at_payment_step; eg: "product_page_no_returns" >> "[Add to cart] Free_30-day_returns · Ships_free_over_$50 [Learn more]"
